frozen_params.scale_note: SCALE ERROR (documented 2026-07-04): beta=0.021545 was fit on D_k=k scale (one unit of damage per full discharge cycle, cross_cell_predictor.py) and is NOT on Palmgren-Miner D scale. The Sobol sweep computes D_total = N_CYCLES × (stress^m / A) — this IS Miner D. Applying beta_k=0.021545 to D_Miner introduces a ~262× underestimate of cycling contribution (verified: beta_Miner fit on same NASA cells gives 3.9–7.9 per cell, mean 5.663). The correctly-scaled beta_Miner predicts ΔSOH_cyc≈0.08–0.25 at fleet-mean conditions — but this is itself inapplicable to Deng NCM fleet (chemistry mismatch: NASA=LCO, Deng=NCM). Consequently the absolute_contribution table below is retracted. The Sobol sensitivity RANKING (T>DoD>C-rate) is scale-invariant and unaffected. Also: eval_B_cross_cell.beta=2730.959 in nasa_degradation_report.json is on a different (incorrect/raw) D scale and must not be used here.

pre_registered_expectation: T > DoD > C-rate  (Edge J.S. et al. 2021, Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys. 23(14):8200, DOI 10.1039/D1CP00359C)

absolute_contribution_RETRACTED.reason: beta_NASA=0.021545 used in this table is on D_k=k scale (not Palmgren-Miner D scale). Applied here with D_total on Miner scale, it underestimates cycling by ~262x. The correctly-scaled beta_Miner (mean 5.663, fit on same NASA LCO cells) cannot be used either: it predicts ΔSOH_cyc=0.08–0.25 on Deng fleet conditions, exceeding observed total fleet fade of 2–9%, confirming LCO beta does not transfer to NCM Deng fleet (chemistry mismatch). No valid absolute cycling/calendar split can be computed without an NCM-specific damage coefficient calibrated on Deng fleet cycling data, which this project does not have. The Sobol sensitivity RANKING (T>DoD>C-rate, ST: 0.749>0.372>0.166) is scale-invariant and remains valid.

retracted_values_for_audit.error: beta on wrong D scale (D_k=k applied to D_Miner); additionally LCO-to-NCM chemistry transfer invalid

caveats: PHYSICS-MODEL sensitivity only — reflects the current model structure (stress_model.py Arrhenius + Basquin + SEI √t), NOT empirical fleet data. Results are only as defensible as the model's functional form.

λ_sei (calendar term) is temperature-invariant in this model: λ·√t does not depend on T, DoD, or C_rate. Temperature enters ONLY via Arrhenius in the cycling stress. Real SEI kinetics are T-dependent (Arrhenius, Ea≈0.5 eV); this analysis UNDERSTATES T sensitivity in calendar-dominated, long-duration regimes.

Does NOT resolve P5's original ask (rank factors from real Deng fleet data). Substituted because fleet is statistically underpowered at n=13: all |ρ|<0.40, LOO R²=−12.83 (see factor_ranking_report.json). The present result shows what the PHYSICS MODEL believes, not what the data confirms.

Analytical single-amplitude model: one full cycle per session at amplitude=DoD_pct, mean temperature T_C, rate C_rate. No rainflow on SOC time-series. Valid for virtual DoE sweep; may differ from real fleet's mixed-amplitude distributions.

β=0.021545, γ=0.5 sourced from cell_to_field_bridge.py (Module 3/4 D scale). eval_B_cross_cell.beta=2730.959 in nasa_degradation_report.json uses the raw fatigue.py D scale and is NOT interchangeable — produces ΔSOH≈484 at max stress.

Calendar term ΔSOH_cal = λ·√t = 0.02639 is constant across all sweep points. It shifts the mean output but does not contribute to output variance. Sobol indices therefore reflect sensitivity of the CYCLING term only. S1+ST sum ≈ 1 for the three factors (constant term adds no variance).

N_cycles=800 is held constant across all C_rate values in this sweep. Physically, higher C_rate typically enables more charge sessions per unit time, which would compound the cycling effect. This sweep isolates per-cycle stress sensitivity only and likely UNDERSTATES C_rate's true real-world impact on annual ΔSOH.
verdict: T > DoD > C-rate confirmed by ST (0.749 > 0.372 > 0.166). Consistent with Edge et al. (2021). NOTE: T sensitivity is through Arrhenius cycling stress only — calendar λ is T-invariant in this model. This Sobol ranking describes sensitivity WITHIN the cycling sub-model only. It does NOT contradict the M2 finding that field degradation is calendar-dominated (deng_degradation_report.json: stress_frac=0%) — the M2 finding is grounded in M2's own NCM fit (gamma_c=3.630 on Deng data), not in any NASA-derived beta. The absolute cycling/calendar split as a percentage CANNOT be computed honestly from this analysis: beta_NASA=0.021545 is on D_k=k scale (not D_Miner scale used here), producing ~262× scale error; the correctly-scaled beta_Miner from NASA LCO cells fails to transfer to Deng NCM fleet due to chemistry mismatch. See absolute_contribution_RETRACTED for details. The sensitivity RANKING (T>DoD>C-rate) is valid and unaffected by the scale error.
